message:
Fix SQLite connection handling in mailbox share integration test

Apply the same explicit connection close pattern used in test_share_export.py
to ensure proper cleanup and prevent "database is locked" errors.

Changes:
- Replaced `with sqlite3.connect()` context manager with explicit try/finally
- Added conn.close() in finally block for guaranteed cleanup
- Added explicit conn.commit() after data writes
- Matches pattern from commit 0d7c463 for consistency across test suite

This ensures the connection is properly closed even if exceptions occur during
test setup, preventing resource leaks and intermittent lock failures on some
platforms (particularly Windows and slow CI runners).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -32,7 +32,8 @@ def _seed_mailbox(db_path: Path, storage_root: Path) -> None:
     (attachments_dir / "bundle.bin").write_bytes(b"B" * 256)
     (attachments_dir / "huge.dat").write_bytes(b"H" * 1024 * 32)
 
-    with sqlite3.connect(db_path) as conn:
+    conn = sqlite3.connect(db_path)
+    try:
         conn.executescript(
             """
             CREATE TABLE projects (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, slug TEXT, human_key TEXT);
@@ -119,6 +120,9 @@ def _seed_mailbox(db_path: Path, storage_root: Path) -> None:
         conn.execute(
             "INSERT INTO agent_links (id, a_project_id, b_project_id) VALUES (1, 1, 1)"
         )
+        conn.commit()
+    finally:
+        conn.close()
 
 
 @pytest.mark.usefixtures("isolated_env")
